candidates.conversion_notes: Clean 4-child conditional dispatch. ParseCommand validates, parent routes to handler based on command. All dataflow parent-mediated. No type info in Stage1 — will default to Any/dict.

candidates.formatter_aggregator_complete: FormatResponse receives operation_result from parent, returns output to parent. Complete path.
candidates.conversion_notes: 6-child conditional dispatch with formatter. ParseAndValidateCommand validates, parent routes to handler, FormatResponse wraps output. All dataflow parent-mediated. SendMessage/GetHistory/CreateChannel/JoinChannel have 'internal leaf access' inputs (channels, messages stores) — these are data_sources, not sibling calls.

candidates.formatter_aggregator_complete: FormatOutput receives action, build_id, status, logs, builds_list, error from parent (sourced from various children). Returns output to parent. Complete path.
candidates.conversion_notes: 10-child conditional dispatch with aggregator. Complex but all parent-mediated. FormatOutput.aggregate role collects from multiple children via parent. Trigger workflow: ParseAndValidateInput -> CheckBuildConstraint -> CreateBuildRecord -> RunCompilation -> RunTests -> PackageArtifacts (sequential within parent). Status/List/Cancel are independent query paths. All dataflow edges are parent<->child. No types in Stage1 — will default to Any/dict. Note: FormatOutput input sources reference multiple children (CreateBuildRecord|QueryBuildStatus|CancelBuild) — this is aggregate input from parent mediation, not sibling calls.

candidates.formatter_aggregator_complete: FormatOutput receives result from RouteCommand, returns output to parent.
candidates.conversion_notes: EXPLICIT ROUTING VIOLATION: RouteCommand behavior says 'Use conditional logic to select which child to call based on parsed_command value'. RouteCommand's dataflow shows direct edges to HandleSendMessage, HandleGetHistory, HandleCreateChannel, HandleJoinChannel — these are sibling calls. Handle*.semantic_outputs.consumer = 'RouteCommand' confirms handler results go back to RouteCommand, not parent. This is a classic routing pattern: ParseAndValidateInput -> RouteCommand -> handlers -> RouteCommand -> FormatOutput. The parent never directly calls the handlers.
candidates.failure_mode: RouteCommand explicitly described as dispatching to handler siblings. Dataflow shows RouteCommand->Handle* edges (sibling orchestration). Handler outputs consumed by RouteCommand, not parent.

candidates.conversion_notes: ABSTRACTION-LEVEL MIXING: This is a 2-level decomposition flattened into a single level. PlaceOrder is both a child of OrderSystem AND the orchestrator of ValidateItemsAndStock, ChargePayment, ReserveInventory, CreateOrderRecord. CancelOrder is both a child AND orchestrator of VerifyOrderNotShipped, RefundPayment, RestoreInventory, UpdateOrderStatus. PlaceOrder.behavior: 'Call ValidateItemsAndStock, ChargePayment, ReserveInventory, CreateOrderRecord in sequence'. CancelOrder.behavior: 'Call VerifyOrderNotShipped, RefundPayment, RestoreInventory, UpdateOrderStatus in sequence'. Dataflow shows PlaceOrder->ValidateItemsAndStock, PlaceOrder->ChargePayment, etc. (sibling orchestration). Sub-child outputs (e.g. ValidateItemsAndStock.validation_result) have consumer=PlaceOrder, not parent. The parent OrderSystem only calls ParseAndValidateInput, PlaceOrder, CancelOrder, TrackOrder directly — it never calls the 8 sub-children. If flattened to parent-mediated, PlaceOrder/CancelOrder become empty orchestrators with no own logic, and the parent must sequence 10+ children for each workflow.
candidates.failure_mode: PlaceOrder and CancelOrder explicitly described as calling their sub-children (ValidateItemsAndStock, ChargePayment, etc.). Dataflow shows PlaceOrder->sub-child edges (sibling orchestration). Sub-child outputs consumed by PlaceOrder/CancelOrder, not parent. This is abstraction-level mixing: 2-level hierarchy flattened to 1 level.
